text: Reuters reports US deploying additional 2,500 Marines and USS Boxer Amphibious Ready Group to Middle East, bringing total US troop presence to ~50,000. The deployment is linked to potential Strait of Hormuz operations. Trump publicly denied sending more troops while Pentagon officials confirmed the deployment — the contradiction highlighted tensions between Trump's messaging and military reality.
verificationNote: Stars and Stripes, Military.com, NPR confirmed the deployment

text: Iran fired two Intermediate-Range Ballistic Missiles at Diego Garcia tonight — reaching approximately 4,000km, double the previously acknowledged range. One failed mid-flight. US destroyer fired SM-3 interceptor at second. Neither hit the base. This is Iran's most distant strike of the war and reveals a previously hidden IRBM capability.
verificationNote: Reported by WSJ citing multiple US officials; consistent with Times of Israel March 21 liveblog. Official confirmation pending from US/UK/Iran.

text: IDF confirmed killing IRGC spokesman Brig. Gen. Ali Mohammad Naini in overnight strikes on Tehran/Karaj — fourth senior Iranian official eliminated this week, following Larijani, Gholamreza Soleimani, and Intelligence Minister Khatib. IRGC has confirmed the death. Naini was publicly speaking just hours before his death.
verificationNote: Confirmed by IDF and IRGC official channels. Reported by Al Jazeera, WION, Daily Sabah.

text: Trump's 'winding down' statement and NATO 'COWARDS' post appear to be contradictory signals. While Trump suggests operational end is near, the Pentagon simultaneously drafted detailed ground force plans including 82nd Airborne deployment scenarios and JSOC nuclear material extraction operations. 2,500 more Marines departed San Diego today.
verificationNote: Trump posts verified via CNBC, Bloomberg. Pentagon ground plans confirmed by CBS News. Marine deployment confirmed by Military.com.

text: Nowruz (Persian New Year) was marked by one of the heaviest air assault days of the war — IDF struck 200+ targets in Tehran including ballistic missile storage, launch sites, UAV facilities, and air defense systems. For the first time, Israel struck Iran's Caspian coast at Bandar Anzali naval base, destroying 5 missile boats. Mojtaba Khamenei issued a written Nowruz message but did not appear on camera.
verificationNote: IDF confirmed strikes on Bandar Anzali. Alma Research Center daily report verified. Khamenei message verified by Al Jazeera.

text: Iran is developing a selective 'vetting system' for Strait of Hormuz passage — friendly nations (India, China, Turkey, Pakistan, Malaysia) are negotiating case-by-case transit. At least 9 ships have already transited Iranian territorial waters. One tanker reportedly paid $2M for passage rights. AIS data shows ~8 non-Iranian vessels detected March 18 (nearly double recent days). Western shipping remains blocked.
verificationNote: Reported by Al Jazeera citing tracking data. AIS vessel data consistent with CNBC Hormuz tracking report.

text: Day 22: Iran fired two IRBMs at Diego Garcia approximately 7 hours after UK PM Starmer authorized US use of the base for strikes on Iranian Hormuz missile sites. One missile failed mid-flight; a US Navy destroyer intercepted the second with an SM-3. No damage or casualties. Demonstrates Iran's ~4,000km IRBM range — double previously claimed capability.
verificationNote: Diego Garcia IRBM launch confirmed by WSJ, CNBC, Times of Israel, ITV. SM-3 intercept reported by multiple US officials.

text: Natanz nuclear enrichment facility struck by US-Israel on Day 22. Iranian Tasnim reports 'no radioactive leakage.' This is the first confirmed strike on the primary centrifuge enrichment complex. Earlier strikes hit auxiliary buildings; today's attack appears to target the main hall. Internet blackout prevents independent BDA.
verificationNote: Natanz strike confirmed by Al Jazeera and Times of Israel liveblogs. 'No radioactive leakage' claim from Tasnim only; not independently verified.

text: Araghchi signals Iran may seek diplomatic exit: 'We seek to end the war permanently' per Al Arabiya. Sets conditions: permanent end to attacks + reparations. Simultaneously reports of CIA-Iranian intelligence backchannel through a third country. First diplomatic signal from Tehran in 22 days of war.
verificationNote: Reported by Al Arabiya — not yet confirmed by official Iranian statement. Iran International also reports backchannel contact.

text: IDF Day 22 BDA summary: 356 total timeline events logged. Caspian Sea campaign confirmed — 4 missile boats and 1 corvette destroyed at Bandar Anzali. Cluster missile struck Rishon Lezion daycare (empty); debris near Al-Aqsa in Jerusalem. Israel choosing not to intercept some cluster bomblets to conserve interceptor supply (90%+ rate maintained).
verificationNote: IDF confirmed Caspian strikes via Lt. Col. Shoshani. Rishon Lezion damage confirmed by Israeli emergency services.

text: Brent crude pulls back to $107.20 (from $110.28) on Day 22. US Treasury's 30-day sanctions waiver for 140M barrels of Iranian crude officially in effect through April 19. Bessent: 'using Iranian barrels against the Iranians.' WTI at $98.23. Goldman Sachs maintains triple-digit oil for 'potentially years' if Hormuz stays blocked.
verificationNote: Oil prices from CNBC/Bloomberg Day 22. Sanctions waiver confirmed by CNBC and WaPo. Goldman projection from CNN Business.

text: Hormuz Blockade Day 19 update: Iran refuses to discuss Hormuz reopening while under attack (Bloomberg). DIA assessment says Iran can maintain blockade 1-6 months. 21 total ships have crossed via Iranian corridor since war began vs 100+/day pre-war. ~40,000 seafarers remain stranded. Trump called NATO 'COWARDS' for refusing to contribute troops to open strait.
verificationNote: DIA blockade assessment from CNN. 21 ships figure from Bloomberg Hormuz tracker. NATO COWARDS post from Bloomberg and Al Jazeera.

displayName: Institute for the Study of War (ISW)
text: ISW: Iran's Katz statement contradicts Trump's wind-down narrative — Israel's defense establishment appears to be driving operational tempo, not Washington. Katz declared attacks will 'increase significantly' within hours of Trump's Truth Social winding down post. Pentagon sources describe 'frustration' with Israeli messaging.
verificationNote: ISW daily assessment citing Katz statement vs Trump post.

text: Oryx confirms: IDF footage of 5 simultaneous strikes on western Iran ballistic missile storage array is verified. Imagery shows secondary explosions consistent with solid-fuel propellant storage. At least 3 distinct storage buildings destroyed. This represents Iran's largest single-session loss of ready-to-fire ballistic missile stockpile.
verificationNote: Consistent with IDF-released footage and Jerusalem Post reporting on strike.

text: Analysis: Pezeshkian's Nowruz 'Islamic Assembly of Middle East' proposal is diplomatically significant but structurally hollow — it implicitly demands US withdrawal as a precondition. The speech signals Tehran knows the war is unsustainable but lacks leverage to end it on acceptable terms. Watch for backchannel activity in the next 72 hours.
verificationNote: Analytical assessment consistent with Pezeshkian's Nowruz address reported by ANI and Al Jazeera.

text: Goldsmith analysis: Araghchi's NBC demand for war 'reparations' and a 'permanent end' rather than a ceasefire marks a hardening of Iran's diplomatic posture. Combined with Katz's escalation signal and Trump's nominal wind-down, the gap between all parties' stated positions is as wide as it has been at any point in Day 22.
verificationNote: Analysis consistent with publicly available Araghchi NBC statements and Katz's media statement.

text: Behravesh: IAEA's 'no radiation' confirmation from Natanz actually serves Iranian diplomatic interests — it removes the nuclear catastrophe narrative while allowing Iran to claim Natanz was hit without triggering global panic. IDF's denial of the Natanz strike is unusual and suggests US may be operating more unilaterally than acknowledged.
verificationNote: Analysis consistent with IAEA statement and IDF denial reported by Jerusalem Post and Al Jazeera.

text: CENTCOM confirms overnight IDF strikes in western Iran destroyed a 'major ballistic missile component manufacturing and storage complex.' DoD says Iran's ready-to-fire ballistic missile inventory is now estimated below 15% of pre-war capacity. Missile attack rate into Israel has dropped ~90% from war-opening pace.
verificationNote: DoD briefing consistent with Hegseth public remarks and CENTCOM statements reported by multiple outlets.
